title: fraxinus velutina subsp. papillosa
intro: Fraxinus velutina subsp. papillosa, also known as the velvet ash, is a subspecies of the desert ash tree native to the southwestern United States. The velvet ash is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The plant's bark and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its analgesic, antiseptic, and astringent properties. In herbal preparations, the velvet ash is often combined with other plants to create teas, tinctures, and salves that are said to promote wound healing, reduce pain, and alleviate respiratory issues.
